title: Clothing boutique ecommerce store
brief_example: Client needed a clothing boutique website with 40 products, Stripe checkout, customer accounts, and order tracking.
scope_summary: Responsive ecommerce site with product catalog, cart, Stripe checkout, admin order panel, customer accounts, and order tracking.
typical_questions: Do products have size or color variants?
Who uploads product photos and descriptions?
Do you need shipping zones and tax rules?
risk_flags: Product variants can increase complexity.
Shipping and tax rules can expand scope.
Admin workflow details must be clarified.
why_relevant: Useful for boutique ecommerce briefs with products, checkout, accounts, and admin needs.

title: Restaurant website with menu and booking
brief_example: Client needed a modern restaurant website with menu, photos, booking form, Google Maps, and contact details.
scope_summary: Five-page marketing website with menu management, gallery, reservation inquiry form, map, and responsive layout.
risk_flags: Online ordering is often requested later.
Poor photo readiness can delay launch.
why_relevant: Useful for small business website briefs with booking and content pages.

title: SaaS landing page and lead funnel
brief_example: Startup needed a SaaS landing page with pricing section, testimonials, waitlist capture, and analytics.
scope_summary: High-converting landing page with responsive sections, form capture, analytics setup, and basic SEO metadata.
risk_flags: Copywriting and conversion strategy can expand scope.
Integrations can be unclear.
why_relevant: Useful for landing pages focused on conversion, lead capture, and launch speed.

title: Custom SaaS tool MVP
brief_example: Client needed a web SaaS MVP with login, workspace dashboard, role-based access, records management, subscription-ready structure, and deployment.
scope_summary: Web SaaS MVP with authentication, dashboard UI, CRUD workflows, user roles, admin controls, basic analytics, deployment, and handoff documentation.
typical_questions: What core workflow must be automated first?
Is an admin panel required for launch?
risk_flags: Role permissions and admin workflows can expand quickly.
Billing, teams, and audit logs should be separate scope unless confirmed.
Data model uncertainty can cause rework.
why_relevant: Useful for SaaS and custom web app briefs with login, dashboards, roles, data workflows, admin tools, and deployment.

title: Document parsing SaaS MVP
brief_example: Client wanted a SaaS tool that uploads PDF invoices, extracts structured fields, lets users review corrections, exports CSV, and stores processing history.
scope_summary: Document parsing web app with upload flow, parsing/OCR or AI extraction pipeline, review UI, validation states, export, history, auth, and deployment.
typical_questions: Which document types must be supported?
Is OCR required or are PDFs text-based?
What accuracy target is acceptable for v1?
Should extraction use AI APIs or deterministic templates?
risk_flags: Parsing accuracy depends heavily on document variety.
OCR, AI API costs, privacy, and edge-case validation must be scoped.
Human review tooling is usually required for reliable MVP output.
why_relevant: Useful for SaaS parser, document automation, PDF extraction, OCR, AI extraction, data validation, and export workflow briefs.

title: Professional portfolio website
brief_example: Freelance photographer needed a portfolio with galleries, about page, inquiry form, and image optimization.
scope_summary: Portfolio website with categorized galleries, image optimization, contact form, and easy content updates.
risk_flags: Large image sets can slow delivery.
CMS expectations should be clarified.
why_relevant: Useful for portfolio and personal brand websites.

title: Personal finance tracker app
brief_example: Client wanted an iOS-first app for income, expense tracking, charts, budget alerts, and future Android support.
scope_summary: Mobile app MVP with local data, income and expense entry, charts, budget alerts, and onboarding.
risk_flags: Bank integrations are high complexity.
Cross-device sync requires backend scope.
why_relevant: Useful for mobile MVPs with charts, alerts, and phased platform needs.

title: Food delivery marketplace MVP
brief_example: Client needed a food delivery app for customers, restaurants, drivers, and admin.
scope_summary: Multi-role delivery MVP with ordering, restaurant menu management, driver assignment, tracking, and admin dashboard.
typical_questions: Which user roles are required for v1?
risk_flags: Multi-role apps are large projects.
Live tracking and payments add major complexity.
why_relevant: Useful for marketplace and delivery app briefs with multiple user roles.

title: Habit tracker app
brief_example: Client wanted a habit tracker with reminders, streaks, calendar view, and simple progress analytics.
scope_summary: Mobile MVP with habit creation, reminders, streaks, calendar tracking, and basic progress charts.
risk_flags: Push notifications require platform setup.
Cloud backup changes backend scope.
why_relevant: Useful for lightweight productivity and reminder app briefs.

title: Social media feed MVP
brief_example: Client wanted an iOS and Android social app MVP with user profiles, image/video posts, feed, likes, comments, follows, and basic moderation.
scope_summary: Cross-platform social MVP with authentication, profiles, media posting, feed, likes/comments, follows, reporting, and admin moderation basics.
typical_questions: Is this iOS, Android, or both?
Do videos need upload, compression, or streaming?
risk_flags: Media upload and storage can increase infrastructure cost.
Moderation and safety requirements must be scoped.
Real-time social features can expand quickly.
why_relevant: Useful for social media app briefs with profiles, feeds, media posts, engagement, and moderation.

title: Chat and messaging app MVP
brief_example: Client needed a mobile messaging MVP with login, one-to-one chat, group chat, media attachments, read receipts, and push notifications.
scope_summary: Mobile chat MVP with auth, conversations, messaging, media attachments, notifications, and basic user settings.
typical_questions: Do chats need moderation or reporting?
How long should message history be stored?
risk_flags: Real-time messaging requires backend reliability.
Encryption, calls, and moderation can significantly expand scope.
why_relevant: Useful for mobile app briefs focused on chat, direct messaging, groups, notifications, and media sharing.

title: Community app for creators
brief_example: Creator wanted a community app with member profiles, topic groups, posts, comments, notifications, and admin announcements.
scope_summary: Community MVP with groups, member profiles, post threads, comments, notifications, announcements, and admin controls.
risk_flags: Membership payments and private permissions add scope.
Community moderation must be clarified.
why_relevant: Useful for community apps with posts, groups, members, notifications, and moderation.

title: Photo and video sharing app
brief_example: Client wanted a creator app with camera capture, photo/video upload, creator profiles, feed, saves, and comments.
scope_summary: Creator content app with camera/media capture, uploads, profiles, feed, saves, comments, and storage rules.
typical_questions: Is video processing or compression needed?
risk_flags: Video processing and storage can increase backend cost.
Editing tools and analytics should be separate phases.
why_relevant: Useful for Instagram/Snapchat-style briefs with media capture, upload, profiles, feeds, and engagement.

title: Casual mobile game MVP
brief_example: Client wanted a 2D mobile game MVP for iOS and Android with core gameplay, levels, scoring, leaderboard, ads, and in-app purchases.
scope_summary: Cross-platform mobile game MVP with core gameplay loop, menus, level/content setup, scoring, save state, leaderboard basics, monetization hooks, analytics basics, and release QA.
typical_questions: Are art, animation, sound, and level content already provided?
How many levels or content packs are included in v1?
Which monetization features are needed for launch?
risk_flags: Custom art, animation, and sound can exceed development scope.
Online multiplayer and real-time gameplay are major scope increases.
Game balancing, device QA, ads, in-app purchases, and app-store compliance need explicit scope.
why_relevant: Useful for mobile game briefs with gameplay mechanics, levels, scoring, leaderboards, monetization, and app-store release needs.

title: Fintech dashboard UX redesign
brief_example: Client needed a fintech web dashboard redesigned in Figma with cleaner navigation and analytics screens.
scope_summary: UX audit, information architecture cleanup, dashboard wireframes, high-fidelity Figma screens, and prototype.
typical_questions: Do you need a full design system?
risk_flags: Screen count can grow quickly.
Research and design system work must be scoped separately.
why_relevant: Useful for UI/UX dashboard redesigns with Figma deliverables.

title: Mobile app MVP UI kit
brief_example: Founder needed 18 mobile screens for a wellness app, including onboarding, dashboard, tracking, and settings.
scope_summary: Mobile app UX flow, wireframes, 18 high-fidelity screens, clickable prototype, and lightweight UI kit.
typical_questions: Do you need developer handoff specs?
risk_flags: Screen count and custom illustrations can increase price.
Developer handoff expectations should be clarified before final delivery.
why_relevant: Useful for mobile app UI/UX design requests.

title: Monthly SEO blog package
brief_example: Agency needed 8 SEO articles per month, 1000 words each, about SEO, social media, and email marketing.
scope_summary: Monthly content package with keyword-aligned article outlines, SEO blog writing, meta titles, and revisions.
risk_flags: Research depth changes effort.
CMS uploading should be scoped separately.
why_relevant: Useful for recurring SEO blog and content writing briefs.

title: Technical SaaS whitepaper
brief_example: B2B SaaS company needed a 3000-word whitepaper explaining API automation benefits for operations teams.
scope_summary: Technical content project with outline, subject research, stakeholder notes, long-form writing, and two revision rounds.
risk_flags: SME access can affect accuracy.
Diagrams and heavy research add scope.
why_relevant: Useful for technical writing and whitepaper briefs.

title: Sales dashboard in Power BI
brief_example: Client needed a sales dashboard using Excel exports, monthly revenue, product performance, and regional trends.
scope_summary: Power BI dashboard with data cleanup, model setup, revenue KPIs, product analysis, and region filters.
risk_flags: Messy source data can expand scope.
Automated refresh requires extra setup.
why_relevant: Useful for dashboard and BI reporting requests.

title: Python reporting automation
brief_example: Client wanted weekly automated reports from CSV files with charts and PDF output.
scope_summary: Python script that ingests CSV files, cleans data, creates charts, and exports weekly PDF reports.
risk_flags: Scheduling and deployment can expand scope.
Unstable CSV format can cause maintenance needs.
why_relevant: Useful for data automation and reporting briefs.

title: Membership website with gated content
brief_example: Coach needed a website with paid members, gated videos, login, and subscription management.
scope_summary: Membership website with public pages, auth, gated resources, subscription payments, and member dashboard.
risk_flags: Video hosting and subscription rules need clarity.
Authentication adds security requirements.
why_relevant: Useful for paid membership and gated content projects.

title: Service booking website
brief_example: Salon needed a booking website with services, staff availability, appointment requests, and SMS reminders.
scope_summary: Service website with service catalog, appointment booking, staff availability, notifications, and admin review.
typical_questions: Do reminders need SMS or email?
risk_flags: Calendar sync and reminders can increase complexity.
Payment and cancellation policy must be scoped.
why_relevant: Useful for appointment booking and service business projects.

title: Brand website UI design
brief_example: Client needed Figma design for a premium services website with homepage, about, services, case studies, and contact.
scope_summary: Website UI design with sitemap, wireframes, five high-fidelity pages, responsive variants, and handoff notes.
risk_flags: Branding and copy gaps can delay UI work.
Responsive variants and interaction states can expand beyond static page design.
why_relevant: Useful for UI design work without implementation.

title: Ecommerce product copywriting
brief_example: Store owner needed product descriptions for 60 skincare products with SEO keywords and consistent brand tone.
scope_summary: Product copy package with SEO-friendly product descriptions, benefits, usage notes, and tone consistency.
risk_flags: Missing product specs can slow writing.
Medical or regulated claims need careful review.
why_relevant: Useful for ecommerce product writing briefs.

title: Inventory analysis dashboard
brief_example: Retailer needed dashboard to analyze stock levels, reorder needs, slow-moving products, and supplier performance.
scope_summary: Inventory analytics dashboard with stock KPIs, reorder alerts, product movement analysis, and supplier views.
risk_flags: Metric definitions must be agreed before build.
Data quality can affect output reliability.
why_relevant: Useful for retail analytics and inventory dashboard projects.

title: Small business workflow automation
brief_example: Client needed to automate lead intake from forms into a spreadsheet and send follow-up emails.
scope_summary: Automation workflow connecting forms, spreadsheet, email notifications, and simple status tracking.
risk_flags: Third-party tool limits can affect reliability.
Credentials and ownership must be clarified.
why_relevant: Useful for automation requests that do not fit the main categories.

title: Online course website
brief_example: Instructor wanted to sell online courses with lessons, student login, quizzes, and payment access.
scope_summary: Course website with public sales pages, student login, course modules, quiz basics, payment access, and admin content upload.
risk_flags: Video hosting and certificates can expand scope.
Assessment rules must be clarified.
why_relevant: Useful for education, course, and gated learning platform briefs.

title: Real estate listing website
brief_example: Brokerage needed a property listing website with searchable homes, agent profiles, inquiry forms, saved listings, and map-based browsing.
scope_summary: Real estate website with listing search, filters, property detail pages, agent pages, map view, lead forms, saved listings, and admin listing management.
typical_questions: Do users need accounts for saved listings?
Is map search included in v1?
Are agent lead routing rules required?
risk_flags: MLS/IDX integrations can require third-party approvals and fees.
Map search, saved listings, and lead routing add backend complexity.
Listing data ownership and update workflow must be clarified.
why_relevant: Useful for property, real estate, listing directory, MLS/IDX, map search, and lead capture website briefs.

title: Healthcare appointment portal
brief_example: Clinic needed a patient-facing website with doctor profiles, service pages, appointment requests, intake forms, and admin review.
scope_summary: Healthcare booking portal with public clinic pages, provider profiles, appointment request flow, intake forms, admin triage, email notifications, and privacy-aware handoff.
typical_questions: Is this appointment request only or real calendar booking?
Will patient health data be stored?
risk_flags: Medical privacy and compliance requirements can materially change scope.
Real calendar availability and reminders require integration work.
Patient intake data should be minimized unless compliance is confirmed.
why_relevant: Useful for clinic, healthcare, doctor appointment, intake form, booking, and privacy-sensitive web portal briefs.

title: Nonprofit donation website
brief_example: Nonprofit needed a refreshed website with campaign pages, donation checkout, volunteer form, impact stories, newsletter signup, and CMS editing.
scope_summary: Nonprofit website with campaign pages, Stripe/PayPal donation flow, volunteer intake, impact story pages, newsletter signup, CMS editing, and analytics setup.
typical_questions: Is donor receipt email automation included?
Who provides campaign copy and imagery?
Does the nonprofit need CMS training?
risk_flags: Recurring donations and receipts add payment workflow complexity.
Content readiness often controls launch timing.
Donor data handling should be kept simple unless CRM integration is confirmed.
why_relevant: Useful for nonprofit, charity, donation, fundraising campaign, volunteer form, and CMS website briefs.

title: Two-sided services marketplace MVP
brief_example: Founder needed a marketplace where customers post jobs, providers create profiles, submit offers, message clients, and admins review disputes.
scope_summary: Two-sided marketplace MVP with customer/provider accounts, provider profiles, job posting, offers, messaging basics, admin moderation, payments-ready architecture, and deployment.
typical_questions: Are payments and escrow included in v1?
What admin dispute tools are needed?
risk_flags: Marketplaces multiply scope because each role has its own workflow.
Escrow, disputes, verification, and messaging should be phased carefully.
Admin tooling must be defined early to avoid operational gaps.
why_relevant: Useful for Upwork-style, Fiverr-style, service marketplace, job posting, provider profile, and multi-role web app briefs.

title: AI chatbot support tool
brief_example: Client wanted a web tool that lets staff upload FAQ documents, create an AI support chatbot, test answers, embed it on a website, and review conversation logs.
scope_summary: AI support tool MVP with document upload, knowledge ingestion, chatbot test console, embeddable widget, conversation history, admin controls, API key configuration, and deployment.
typical_questions: Which AI provider should be used?
What file types must be ingested?
Are privacy or retention policies required?
risk_flags: AI answer quality depends on source content and retrieval design.
Conversation privacy, hallucination handling, and fallback wording must be scoped.
Embedding widgets and analytics can expand launch scope.
why_relevant: Useful for AI chatbot, RAG support tool, document upload, embeddable assistant, conversation logs, and AI SaaS MVP briefs.

title: Fitness coaching mobile app
brief_example: Coach needed a mobile app for workout plans, exercise videos, habit check-ins, progress photos, reminders, and client messaging.
scope_summary: Fitness coaching app MVP with user onboarding, workout plans, exercise library, video links, check-ins, progress tracking, push reminders, and coach-client messaging basics.
typical_questions: Are videos hosted inside the app or linked externally?
Do coaches create custom plans per client?
Is Apple Health or Google Fit integration needed?
risk_flags: Video hosting, subscriptions, and health integrations can increase scope.
Personalized coaching workflows need clear admin tools.
Health claims and data privacy should be handled carefully.
why_relevant: Useful for fitness, wellness, coaching, workout plan, habit check-in, and mobile health app briefs.

title: Event ticketing mobile app
brief_example: Organizer wanted an event app where users browse events, buy tickets, receive QR codes, check in at the venue, and get push updates.
scope_summary: Event ticketing MVP with event listings, ticket purchase flow, QR ticket generation, attendee wallet, organizer check-in tool, push notifications, and admin event management.
typical_questions: Do refunds or transfers need support?
risk_flags: Payments, QR validation, refunds, and offline check-in add operational risk.
Seat maps and ticket transfers should be separate scope unless confirmed.
App store timing can affect event launch deadlines.
why_relevant: Useful for event, ticketing, QR code, check-in scanner, organizer admin, and mobile payment app briefs.

title: Driver dispatch mobile app
brief_example: Logistics company needed a driver app with assigned jobs, route notes, proof-of-delivery photos, status updates, and dispatcher dashboard.
scope_summary: Logistics MVP with driver login, assigned deliveries, job status updates, proof-of-delivery capture, route notes, push notifications, and dispatcher/admin web dashboard.
typical_questions: Are signatures required for delivery proof?
Does dispatch integrate with existing software?
How many driver roles or regions are needed?
risk_flags: Live tracking, offline sync, and integrations can significantly expand backend scope.
Proof-of-delivery storage has privacy and retention considerations.
Dispatcher workflows must be mapped before development.
why_relevant: Useful for logistics, delivery driver, dispatch, proof-of-delivery, route tracking, and operations mobile app briefs.

title: Educational quiz app
brief_example: Tutor wanted a mobile app for students to practice quizzes, track scores, unlock lessons, receive reminders, and review explanations.
scope_summary: Education app MVP with student onboarding, topic library, quiz engine, explanations, progress tracking, reminders, and lightweight content admin.
typical_questions: How many topics and questions are included at launch?
Do students need accounts and cloud sync?
Is payment for premium lessons included?
risk_flags: Large question banks create content workload outside development.
Timed tests, subscriptions, and analytics can expand scope.
Content admin needs careful design if non-technical tutors manage lessons.
why_relevant: Useful for education, quiz, e-learning, student progress, question bank, and lesson app briefs.

title: SaaS design system foundation
brief_example: B2B SaaS team needed a Figma design system with tokens, core components, dashboard patterns, responsive states, and handoff documentation.
scope_summary: Design system foundation with visual audit, token setup, component library, dashboard patterns, responsive variants, interaction states, and developer handoff notes.
typical_questions: Is an existing brand guide available?
Will this connect to an existing code library?
How many product screens should be refactored?
risk_flags: Component count can expand quickly.
Accessibility, documentation, and code alignment should be scoped separately.
Design system work requires stakeholder review cycles.
why_relevant: Useful for Figma design systems, component libraries, SaaS UI foundations, tokens, accessibility, and developer handoff briefs.

title: Ecommerce checkout UX redesign
brief_example: Store owner needed checkout UX redesigned to reduce cart abandonment, improve mobile flow, and clarify shipping/payment steps.
scope_summary: Checkout UX project with funnel review, wireframes, mobile-first checkout screens, error states, trust elements, and developer handoff annotations.
typical_questions: How many checkout steps can be changed?
Are copy and trust badges included?
risk_flags: Platform limitations may restrict design changes.
Checkout analytics and A/B testing are separate implementation work.
Payment and shipping edge cases require careful state design.
why_relevant: Useful for ecommerce UX, checkout redesign, conversion optimization, mobile commerce, and cart abandonment design briefs.

title: Launch email sequence
brief_example: Founder needed a 7-email launch sequence for a new SaaS product, including welcome, education, objection handling, trial conversion, and final reminder emails.
scope_summary: Email copy package with positioning review, campaign outline, 7-email sequence, subject lines, preview text, CTA recommendations, and one revision round.
typical_questions: Is this for trial users or cold leads?
What offer or deadline drives conversion?
Will emails be loaded into an ESP?
risk_flags: Weak positioning can make email copy generic.
Segmentation and automation setup should be scoped separately.
Compliance requirements for cold email must be respected.
why_relevant: Useful for email marketing, launch campaigns, SaaS onboarding, nurture sequences, and conversion copywriting briefs.

title: Landing page conversion copy
brief_example: Startup needed landing page copy for a new AI productivity product, including hero messaging, feature sections, social proof, FAQs, and CTA copy.
scope_summary: Conversion copy project with message strategy, page outline, hero copy, benefits, feature sections, objection handling, FAQs, CTA copy, and revision round.
typical_questions: Who is the primary customer segment?
Are testimonials or proof points available?
Will the writer also create wireframe recommendations?
risk_flags: Undefined positioning can require strategy work before copy.
Testimonials, proof, and product specifics affect copy strength.
SEO research and wireframing should be separate if needed.
why_relevant: Useful for landing page copy, startup messaging, product positioning, conversion copy, and SaaS marketing briefs.

title: Customer churn analysis
brief_example: Subscription business needed analysis of customer churn patterns, cohort retention, cancellation reasons, and recommendations to reduce churn.
scope_summary: Analytics project with data audit, cleaning, cohort retention analysis, churn segmentation, cancellation reason analysis, dashboard/report, and recommended actions.
typical_questions: What customer and billing data is available?
Should this be a report or dashboard?
risk_flags: Incomplete customer history can limit analysis quality.
Predictive modeling is separate from descriptive churn analysis.
Business definitions must be agreed before calculations.
why_relevant: Useful for churn, retention, subscription analytics, cohort analysis, customer segmentation, and business recommendations briefs.

title: CRM data cleanup and migration
brief_example: Company needed messy customer spreadsheets cleaned, deduplicated, standardized, mapped to CRM fields, and prepared for import.
scope_summary: Data cleanup project with source audit, deduplication, field normalization, validation rules, import mapping, sample QA, and migration handoff file.
typical_questions: How many records and columns are involved?
Is import execution included or only preparation?
risk_flags: Messy source data can require iterative cleanup.
Incorrect duplicate rules can damage CRM quality.
Import execution and rollback planning should be scoped separately.
why_relevant: Useful for data cleaning, CRM migration, spreadsheet cleanup, deduplication, field mapping, and import preparation briefs.

title: CRM lead routing automation
brief_example: Sales team needed new website leads routed into CRM, assigned by region, tagged by source, notified in Slack, and followed up by email.
scope_summary: CRM automation workflow with form-to-CRM capture, lead source tagging, region-based assignment, Slack notifications, follow-up email trigger, error handling, and documentation.
typical_questions: Which CRM and form tools are used?
risk_flags: CRM API limits and field mismatches can affect reliability.
Assignment rules must be documented before implementation.
Credential ownership and monitoring should be clarified.
why_relevant: Useful for CRM automation, lead routing, Zapier/Make/API workflows, Slack alerts, and sales operations automation briefs.
